name: Admin
description: Administrative control plane — manage users, roles, RBAC scopes, billing limits, payment plans, extension installs, LLM model configuration, and tenant-wide settings.

x-sdl='entity-list'). NO legacy {users:[dict],total} wrapper.

A single user as a canonical SDL entity (kind='user'): id=imperal_id,

tools.name: reset_conversation
tools.description: Reset (clear) a user's chat history and conversational state — history, session memory, skeleton caches — and restart their session. Money, usage, billing, and installed apps are PRESERVED. With NO user_id/email this resets the CALLER's own conversation (available to any user). With a user_id or email it resets THAT user (ADMIN only). Use when a user says 'clear my history', 'start over', 'reset my chat', or an admin asks to reset a specific user. Confirmation is required before it runs.

params_schema.description:
Reset (clear) a specific user's conversation. A target is REQUIRED — pass
user_id or email; the tool NEVER silently defaults to the caller (for a
genuine self-reset the kernel passes the caller's own imperal_id).

user_id.description: imperal_id of the user to reset (admin only for another user)
user_id.title: User Id

email.description: email of the user to reset (admin only for another user)
email.title: Email

params_schema.title: ResetConvParams

return_schema.description:
A single user as a canonical SDL entity (kind='user'): id=imperal_id,
title=display name. Gateway fields are kept verbatim for rendering.
return_schema.title: UserRecord

params_schema.title: UpdateUserLimitsParams

return_schema.description:

return_schema.title: UserRecord

tools.name: list_scopes
tools.description: List all defined scopes.

params_schema.description: Filter scopes.
params_schema.title: ListScopesParams

ScopeRecord.description:
A single scope as a canonical SDL entity (kind='scope').

Scopes arrive in two shapes across the surface:
  * a full gateway dict {name, resource, action, source, source_id,
    is_system, display_name, description, id};
  * a BARE string ('billing:read') — the legacy ScopeListResponse declared
    ``scopes: list[str]``.
The before-validator wraps a bare string into a canonical entity so either
shape resolves to the same SDL entity (id=title=scope string).
ScopeRecord.title: ScopeRecord

return_schema.description:
list_scopes return shape — a REAL sdl.EntityList[ScopeRecord] (items=[...]).
Items may be gateway dicts or bare strings (ScopeRecord wraps both).
NO legacy {scopes:[str],total} wrapper.
return_schema.title: ScopeListResponse

tools.description: Create a scope in resource:action format.

params_schema.description: Create a scope in resource:action format.
params_schema.title: CreateScopeParams

return_schema.description:
A single scope as a canonical SDL entity (kind='scope').

Scopes arrive in two shapes across the surface:
  * a full gateway dict {name, resource, action, source, source_id,
    is_system, display_name, description, id};
  * a BARE string ('billing:read') — the legacy ScopeListResponse declared
    ``scopes: list[str]``.
The before-validator wraps a bare string into a canonical entity so either
shape resolves to the same SDL entity (id=title=scope string).
return_schema.title: ScopeRecord

tools.name: delete_scope
tools.description: Delete a scope by name or ID.

params_schema.description: Delete a scope.
params_schema.title: DeleteScopeParams

return_schema.description:

  * a BARE string ('billing:read') — the legacy ScopeListResponse declared

The before-validator wraps a bare string into a canonical entity so either
shape resolves to the same SDL entity (id=title=scope string).
return_schema.title: ScopeRecord

tools.description: View audit log. Default last 24h, max 50 entries.

AuditEntryRecord.description:
A single audit-log entry as a canonical SDL entity (kind='audit_entry').

Gateway entry dict keys (verified vs panels_audit._build_entry_expanded /
build_audit): id, created_at|timestamp, actor|actor_id, action, target_type,
target_id, source, ip|ip_address, details|message, before_state, after_state.
id = entry id (falls back to timestamp); title = action.
AuditEntryRecord.title: AuditEntryRecord

return_schema.description:
audit_log return shape — a REAL sdl.EntityList[AuditEntryRecord]
(items=[...]). The handler keeps its observability scalars as extra typed
fields (period/showing/truncated/hours) — additive on the pydantic
EntityList. NO legacy {entries:[dict],total,period} wrapper.
return_schema.title: AuditLogResponse

ExtensionRecord.description:
A single registered extension as a canonical SDL entity
(kind='extension'): id=app_id, title=display_name (or name).

Registry app dict keys (verified vs panels_extensions._build_expanded_content
and the list-item builder) kept verbatim. ``version`` stays a plain field
(the registry returns it as a string) rather than mixing the Versioned facet,
matching the additive non-breaking SDL migration policy.
ExtensionRecord.title: ExtensionRecord

return_schema.description:
list_extensions return shape — a REAL sdl.EntityList[ExtensionRecord]
(items=[...]). NO legacy {extensions:[dict],total} wrapper.
return_schema.title: ExtensionsListResponse

tools.name: purge_app
tools.description: Permanently purge ANY app from the ENTIRE system — files on the worker, every DB row, Redis caches, the Registry entry, and the marketplace listing. Admin-only. Pass confirm_name equal to the EXACT app_id. Set force=true to purge an app that is still active. THIS CANNOT BE UNDONE.

params_schema.description: Permanently purge an app from the ENTIRE system. Admin-only, irreversible.

confirm_name.title: Confirm Name

force.description: Purge even if the app is still active
force.title: Force

payload keyed by app_id (update_extension_config, update_skeleton_ttl,

tools.name: system_health
tools.description: Check platform health.

params_schema.description:
Federal V17 placeholder for handlers that take no parameters.

`@chat.function` MUST declare a Pydantic params model — this is the
canonical empty model reused by read-only handlers (list_*, get_*,
*_health) that don't need any input.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

return_schema.description:
system_health return shape — a single system-health entity
(kind='systemhealth').

The handler returns EXACTLY ``{auth_gateway, registry}`` (verified vs
handlers_system.fn_system_health: ``data=results`` where results has only
those two keys). The old SystemHealthResponse keys ``overall`` and
``services`` were PHANTOM and are removed.
return_schema.title: SystemHealthRecord

tools.name: list_rules
tools.description: List all automation rules.

params_schema.description:
Federal V17 placeholder for handlers that take no parameters.

`@chat.function` MUST declare a Pydantic params model — this is the
canonical empty model reused by read-only handlers (list_*, get_*,
*_health) that don't need any input.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

RuleRecord.description:
A single automation rule as a canonical SDL entity (kind='rule').

Rule dicts come from the gateway automations API (/v1/automations/...).
id = rule id (or rule_id); title = name|description|prompt. Known keys kept
verbatim; complex/optional fields are Optional[Any]. delete/pause/resume key
on ``rule_id`` (an int), so it is preserved alongside ``id``.
RuleRecord.title: RuleRecord

return_schema.description:
list_rules return shape — a REAL sdl.EntityList[RuleRecord] (items=[...]).
The handler keeps ``my_rules_count`` as an extra typed field (additive on
the pydantic EntityList). NO legacy {rules:[dict],total,my_rules_count}
wrapper.
return_schema.title: AdminRulesListResponse

tools.name: create_rule
tools.description: Create automation from natural language.

params_schema.description: Create automation from natural language.
params_schema.title: RulePromptParams

return_schema.description:
Receipt entity for rule write/destructive verbs that return a small
payload keyed by rule_id (create_rule, delete_rule, pause_rule, resume_rule)
— kind='rule'. Keeps each observed receipt key verbatim
(rule payload / deleted / paused / resumed / rule_id). Reuse rather than
inventing a per-verb receipt.
return_schema.title: RuleActionReceipt

tools.name: get_panel_data
tools.description: Get panel Declarative UI data for admin extension.

params_schema.description:
Federal V17 placeholder for handlers that take no parameters.

`@chat.function` MUST declare a Pydantic params model — this is the
canonical empty model reused by read-only handlers (list_*, get_*,
*_health) that don't need any input.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

tools.name: save_llm_config
tools.description: Save LLM provider/model config to Redis Config Store.

params_schema.description: Save LLM provider/model configuration.

api_key.description: API key (write-only, leave blank to keep)
api_key.title: Api Key

base_url.description: Custom base URL for OpenAI-compatible providers
base_url.title: Base Url

step_reclassify_model.description: Two-Phase Sprint 1 per-step re-classifier model (was env IMPERAL_STEP_RECLASSIFY_MODEL). Default Sonnet 4.6 — reliable arg-binding on large prior step data.
step_reclassify_model.title: Step Reclassify Model

tool_picker_model.description: Chain executor LLM tool picker (action_plan=null disambiguation path) model
tool_picker_model.title: Tool Picker Model

action_narrator_model.description: Action data narrator (post-tool prose) model override
action_narrator_model.title: Action Narrator Model

step_reclassify_enabled.description: BOOLEAN. Two-Phase Sprint 1 per-step re-classifier ON/OFF. When True, each write/destructive chain step runs through a focused Sonnet LLM that binds args from prior step results before dispatch (was env IMPERAL_STEP_RECLASSIFY_ENABLED). Default True (Sprint 2 production). Disable for cost optimization or A/B testing legacy _apply_target_hint_post_ref + _verify_user_named_container_intent path. Reads at orchestration/chain_executor.py:_read_step_reclassify_flag.
step_reclassify_enabled.title: Step Reclassify Enabled

judge_enabled.description: BOOLEAN. Federal prose-judge anti-fabrication Gate 6 ON/OFF (was env IMPERAL_JUDGE_ENABLED). When True, every narrator output is reviewed by a judge LLM that flags fabricated entities/IDs. Default False (opt-in). Reads at responses/judge.py:is_judge_enabled.
judge_enabled.title: Judge Enabled

set_extension_override.description: Extension ID to set override for
set_extension_override.title: Set Extension Override

reset_extension_override.description: Extension ID to remove override for
reset_extension_override.title: Reset Extension Override

narration_history_limit.description: UNIT: turns. How many recent conversation turns the narrator LLM sees when composing replies. Higher = better continuity, more tokens per call. Default 12. Affects narration prompt context window in chain_renderer.py.
narration_history_limit.title: Narration History Limit

confirmation_card_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens cap for the LLM that summarizes write/destructive actions on confirmation cards. Default 300. Higher = more detailed card text, slightly more cost per confirmation. Reads at safety/confirmation.py:340.
confirmation_card_tokens.title: Confirmation Card Tokens

judge_digest_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Cap on audit-judge digest of tool results before LLM critique runs. Default 8000. Higher = judge sees more raw data, more cost per audit. Reads at responses/judge.py:151.
judge_digest_chars.title: Judge Digest Chars

chain_prior_step_max_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Per-step truncation cap of prior step output that subsequent chain steps see in their context. Default 8000. Higher = next step has more context, more LLM tokens. Reads at chain_executor.py:_summarise_prior_steps.
chain_prior_step_max_chars.title: Chain Prior Step Max Chars

chain_prior_total_max_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Total budget across ALL prior-step summaries fed to current step (max sum of per-step caps). Default 64000. Higher = richer cross-step context but bigger prompt. Reads at chain_executor.py:_summarise_prior_steps.
chain_prior_total_max_chars.title: Chain Prior Total Max Chars

hub_dispatch_max_depth.description: UNIT: count. Max nested inter-extension delegation depth a single turn may chain (root excluded; N = N nested hops). Default 6. 0 disables multi-step chains entirely. Reads at orchestration/hub_dispatch_handler.py:_check_depth.
hub_dispatch_max_depth.title: Hub Dispatch Max Depth

automation_main_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for automation rule plan-parser LLM that decodes user prompt into structured rule definition. Default 4096. Reads at activities/automation.py:358.
automation_main_max_tokens.title: Automation Main Max Tokens

automation_condition_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for automation condition-eval LLM that decides whether an event matches a rule condition. Default 50 (small — yes/no answers). Reads at activities/automation.py:448.
automation_condition_max_tokens.title: Automation Condition Max Tokens

intent_classifier_planner_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for intent classifier (Haiku) that routes every user turn. Default 4096. Includes chain plans + action_plans. Reads at hub/intent_classifier.py:881.
intent_classifier_planner_max_tokens.title: Intent Classifier Planner Max Tokens

prose_judge_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for prose-judge LLM (federal Gate 6 anti-fabrication review of every narrator output). Default 4096. Reads at narration/prose_judge.py:234.
prose_judge_max_tokens.title: Prose Judge Max Tokens

system_handlers_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for kernel system_chat handler (the LLM that answers free-form 'what can you do' / capability questions). Default 4096. Reads at pipeline/system_handlers.py:300.
system_handlers_max_tokens.title: System Handlers Max Tokens

responses_judge_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for audit-judge LLM (federal post-action review that records pass/fail in audit ledger). Default 4096. Reads at responses/judge.py:163.
responses_judge_max_tokens.title: Responses Judge Max Tokens

rule_engine_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for rule-engine eval LLM (one-shot trigger/condition matchers). Default 50. Reads at services/rule_engine.py:182.
rule_engine_max_tokens.title: Rule Engine Max Tokens

routing_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for classifier/routing LLM (every user turn). Default 4096 (was structured_gen 1024 — truncated 6+ step chain JSON). Raise to 8000+ for reliable 10-step chain planning. Reads at hub/intent_classifier.py:_call_structured_gen via purpose=routing.
routing_max_tokens.title: Routing Max Tokens

execution_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for extension dispatch LLM (BYOLLM router inside @chat.function tool routing). Default inherits from quality_ceiling_tokens. Reads at llm/provider.py purpose=execution.
execution_max_tokens.title: Execution Max Tokens

navigate_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for hub navigator LLM (offer/clarify follow-up prose). Default inherits from quality_ceiling_tokens. Reads at llm/provider.py purpose=navigate.
navigate_max_tokens.title: Navigate Max Tokens

chain_narrative_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for chain narrator LLM (multi-step result rendering). Default 8000 (Sprint 2 fix #12 — long mail body markdown). Reads at hub/chain_renderer.py purpose=chain_narrative.
chain_narrative_max_tokens.title: Chain Narrative Max Tokens

judge_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for federal prose-judge LLM (anti-fab Gate 6). Default 4096. Distinct from prose_judge_max_tokens (narration/prose_judge.py); this is responses/judge.py LLM. Reads at llm/provider.py purpose=judge.
judge_max_tokens.title: Judge Max Tokens

conversational_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for conversational LLM (chitchat / empty-apps fallback). Default 4096. Reads at hub/conversational.py purpose=conversational.
conversational_max_tokens.title: Conversational Max Tokens

step_reclassify_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for Two-Phase Sprint 1 per-step re-classifier LLM (Sonnet) that binds args from prior step data. Default 8000 (Sprint 2 fix #20 — long mail.body markdown synthesis from web-tools 3-5KB JSON). Reads at hub/step_classifier.py:_call_provider.
step_reclassify_max_tokens.title: Step Reclassify Max Tokens

tool_picker_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for chain_executor LLM tool picker (action_plan=null disambiguation path). Default 1024. Reads at orchestration/chain_executor.py:_llm_pick_tool_for_ext.
tool_picker_max_tokens.title: Tool Picker Max Tokens

chain_arg_refs_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for chain $REF formatter LLM (BUG-M JSON-dict → markdown narration for content-shaped fields like mail.body, notes.content_text). Default 2000. Reads at orchestration/chain_arg_refs.py:format_complex_content_fields_async.
chain_arg_refs_max_tokens.title: Chain Arg Refs Max Tokens

semantic_verifier_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for semantic verifier LLM (post-action yes/no schema-validity check). Default 128 (binary answer). Reads at safety/semantic_verifier.py:191.
semantic_verifier_max_tokens.title: Semantic Verifier Max Tokens

action_narrator_max_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. max_tokens for action data narrator LLM (was env IMPERAL_ACTION_NARRATOR_MAX_TOKENS). Default 1024. Reads at workflows/action_data_narrator.py:42.
action_narrator_max_tokens.title: Action Narrator Max Tokens

default_max_response_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. Default max_tokens for chat narration in new user accounts. Default 1024. Higher = longer detailed answers, more cost per response. Reads at kctx.max_response_tokens.
default_max_response_tokens.title: Default Max Response Tokens

default_max_tool_rounds.description: UNIT: count. How many sequential tool-call rounds the SDK chat handler may execute per turn (chain depth ceiling). Default 10. Higher = deeper auto-iteration, more cost. Reads at SDK chat/handler.py:252.
default_max_tool_rounds.title: Default Max Tool Rounds

default_routing_context.description: UNIT: turns. How many recent conversation turns the classifier LLM (Haiku, every turn) sees for context. Default 20. Higher = better anaphora resolution, more tokens. Reads at kctx.routing_context.
default_routing_context.title: Default Routing Context

default_kav_max_retries.description: UNIT: retries. How many times KAV (Kernel Action Validator) retries a tool call before reporting fail. Default 2. Higher = more resilient on transient errors, slower failure surface. Reads at kctx.kav_max_retries.
default_kav_max_retries.title: Default Kav Max Retries

default_confirmation_enabled.description: UNIT: boolean. Whether new users get write/destructive 2-step confirmation cards by default. Default false (admin tenants typically opt in via Settings). Reads at kctx.confirmation_enabled.
default_confirmation_enabled.title: Default Confirmation Enabled

narrator_structured_data_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Cap on JSON-serialized ActionResult.data shown to single-step narrator. Default 8000. Higher = narrator sees more raw tool data, can enumerate more items (e.g. show all 50 emails instead of first 5 + placeholders). Reads at chain_renderer.py:_synth_chain_prose.
narrator_structured_data_chars.title: Narrator Structured Data Chars

default_max_result_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. Cap on per-tool response/output text shown to narrator prose block (distinct from cross-step context cap and JSON data cap). Default 3000 tokens. Higher = narrator sees more verbose tool outputs.
default_max_result_tokens.title: Default Max Result Tokens

list_truncate_items.description: UNIT: count. Max items rendered when $REF resolver shows a list as markdown bullets/table rows. Default 50. Higher = longer tables in chat, more output tokens. Reads at chain_arg_refs.py:_md_target_list.
list_truncate_items.title: List Truncate Items

classifier_fact_ledger_window.description: UNIT: turns. How many recent assistant turns get their data_facts_json (FACTS:) rendered into the classifier prompt for cross-turn anaphora resolution. Default 20 (was hardcoded 5). Higher = better long-conversation memory ('какие письма я отправлял пару турнов назад'), more classifier tokens per call. Cap mirrors SessionMemory.MAX_TURNS=50 with headroom. Reads at hub/classifier/input_builder.py:build_classifier_input.
classifier_fact_ledger_window.title: Classifier Fact Ledger Window

chain_max_refs_per_args.description: UNIT: count. Federal I-REF-CAP-PER-ARGS (2026-05-28). Max $REF tokens allowed inside a single ActionPlan.args payload. Default 20. resolve_arg_refs counts $REF substrings in the JSON-serialised args BEFORE the resolver loop; exceeding the cap returns REF_COUNT_EXCEEDS_CAP failure (caller aborts via UNRESOLVED_REFS). Anti-spam guard against runaway $REF blobs. Reads at orchestration/chain_arg_refs.py:resolve_arg_refs.
chain_max_refs_per_args.title: Chain Max Refs Per Args

cross_turn_max_refs.description: UNIT: turns. Federal I-REF-CAP-CROSS-TURN (2026-05-28). Max depth of cross-turn $REF lookup ($REF:prior_turn[-N].<app>...) against SessionMemory.turns. Default 5 (= legacy _CROSS_TURN_MAX_BACK). Lower = tighter context isolation; higher = deeper conversation memory for refs. Bound applied BEFORE len(SM) check, so admin tightening fires even on long histories. Reads at orchestration/chain_arg_refs.py:_resolve_explicit_cross_turn.
cross_turn_max_refs.title: Cross Turn Max Refs

classifier_data_facts_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Federal I-NO-HARDCODED-DIGEST-CAPS (2026-05-29). Per-tool-call cap on the classifier-facing data_facts_json stored in each SessionMemory turn digest (was hardcoded 1500). This is what the classifier SEES of a prior tool result in the FACTS section. Higher = classifier sees more of large lists/results for cross-turn references, more classifier tokens. Reads at core/session_memory.py:build_turn_digest_from_er.
classifier_data_facts_chars.title: Classifier Data Facts Chars

cross_turn_facts_full_cap_bytes.description: UNIT: bytes. Federal I-NO-HARDCODED-DIGEST-CAPS (2026-05-29). Cap on the UNTRUNCATED data_facts_full_json stored per tool call (was hardcoded 50000 / 50KB). This is the source the cross-turn $REF resolver pipes into note/email content. Higher = larger prior results (long task lists, reports) can be put verbatim into a note or email; payloads over the cap are dropped and the resolver returns cross_turn_data_exceeds_cap. Reads at core/session_memory.py:build_turn_digest_from_er.
cross_turn_facts_full_cap_bytes.title: Cross Turn Facts Full Cap Bytes

classifier_turn_facts_agg_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Federal I-NO-HARDCODED-DIGEST-CAPS (2026-05-29). Per-TURN aggregate cap on total data_facts_json across all tool calls in one turn (was hardcoded 3000). Binding constraint for multi-tool turns — re-trims pro-rata when exceeded. Raise together with classifier_data_facts_chars for large-context work. Reads at core/session_memory.py:build_turn_digest_from_er.
classifier_turn_facts_agg_chars.title: Classifier Turn Facts Agg Chars

quality_ceiling_tokens.description: UNIT: tokens. Federal hard cap on max_tokens for ANY LLM call regardless of per-purpose setting. Default 50000. Protects against cost runaway. Reads at llm/provider.py:create_message.
quality_ceiling_tokens.title: Quality Ceiling Tokens

string_truncate_chars.description: UNIT: characters. Cap on message/response preview length stored in SessionMemory turn digest (was hardcoded 1500). Default 1500. Higher = richer history context but more Redis storage. Reads at core/session_memory.py:_truncate_preview.
string_truncate_chars.title: String Truncate Chars

history_ttl_days.description: UNIT: days. SessionMemory Redis TTL (was hardcoded 1 day / 86400s). Default 1 day. Higher = users keep more conversation history but more Redis storage. Reads via IMPERAL_HISTORY_TTL_SECONDS env at core/session_memory.py:_history_ttl_seconds (env set on worker boot).
history_ttl_days.title: History Ttl Days

params_schema.title: SaveLlmConfigParams

return_schema.description:
Receipt entity for save_llm_config (kind='llmconfig').

save_llm_config returns one of three observed shapes (verified vs
handlers_llm.fn_save_llm_config): the generic save
``{saved, tenant_defaults_updated, config}``, the override-set
``{override, model}``, or the override-reset ``{reset}``. Every observed key
is kept verbatim so $REF / panels read the same data. id = model (when an
override model is set) else a stable 'llm_config' handle.
return_schema.title: LLMConfigReceipt

tools.name: billing_overview
tools.description: Show billing plans, pricing tiers, and platform summary.

params_schema.description:
Federal V17 placeholder for handlers that take no parameters.

`@chat.function` MUST declare a Pydantic params model — this is the
canonical empty model reused by read-only handlers (list_*, get_*,
*_health) that don't need any input.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

PlanRecord.description:
A single billing plan as a canonical SDL entity (kind='plan').

billing_overview builds each plan item with EXACTLY these keys (verified vs
handlers_billing.fn_billing_overview ``plan_summary``):
{name, price, interval, ai_tokens, tool_calls}. id=title=name.
PlanRecord.title: PlanRecord

return_schema.description:
billing_overview return shape — a REAL sdl.EntityList[PlanRecord] whose
items are the plans, PLUS the platform scalar summary carried as extra typed
fields (EntityList is a pydantic BaseModel, so additive fields are allowed).

The handler must return:
    data={"items": [...plan dicts...], "total": <plans_count>,
          "plans_count": <int>, "wallets_active": <int>,
          "stream_events_total": <int>}
NO legacy {plans:[dict],...} wrapper. (``plans_count`` and ``total`` are the
same count; keep both for backward field-name continuity.)
return_schema.title: BillingOverviewResponse

tools.name: list_user_balances
tools.description: List all user wallet balances with totals.

params_schema.description:
Federal V17 placeholder for handlers that take no parameters.

`@chat.function` MUST declare a Pydantic params model — this is the
canonical empty model reused by read-only handlers (list_*, get_*,
*_health) that don't need any input.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

UserBalanceRecord.description:
get_user_balance / list_user_balances wallet entity (kind='userbalance').

``balance`` is a token count (int), NOT a currency Decimal, so the
``money.balance`` (Balanced) facet is deliberately NOT mixed in to avoid
int->Decimal type drift; kept as the existing plain int field.

list_user_balances wallet items use only ``{user_id, balance}``;
get_user_balance additionally returns ``available``, ``holds``,
``holds_total`` — all kept verbatim below.
UserBalanceRecord.title: UserBalanceRecord

return_schema.description:
list_user_balances return shape — a REAL sdl.EntityList[UserBalanceRecord].
The handler keeps the platform scalars ``total_users`` and
``total_tokens_in_circulation`` as extra typed fields below (EntityList is a
pydantic BaseModel, so additive fields are allowed). NO legacy wrapper.
return_schema.title: UserBalancesResponse

tools.name: get_user_balance
tools.description: Get token balance for a specific user including active holds.

params_schema.description: Look up a specific user's token balance.

user_id.description: Canonical imperal_id of the user — format `imp_u_XXXXXXXX`. NOT an email. If you only have an email, call get_user_by_email or list_users first to resolve the imperal_id, then pass that value here. Passing an email creates an orphan wallet key that no other service reads.
user_id.title: User Id

params_schema.title: UserBalanceParams

return_schema.description:
get_user_balance / list_user_balances wallet entity (kind='userbalance').

``balance`` is a token count (int), NOT a currency Decimal, so the
``money.balance`` (Balanced) facet is deliberately NOT mixed in to avoid
int->Decimal type drift; kept as the existing plain int field.

list_user_balances wallet items use only ``{user_id, balance}``;
get_user_balance additionally returns ``available``, ``holds``,
``holds_total`` — all kept verbatim below.
return_schema.title: UserBalanceRecord

tools.name: adjust_balance
tools.description: Credit or deduct tokens. Positive=credit, negative=deduct.

params_schema.description: Credit or deduct tokens from a user wallet.

user_id.description: Canonical imperal_id of the target user — format `imp_u_XXXXXXXX`. NOT an email. If you only have an email, call get_user_by_email or list_users first to resolve the imperal_id, then pass that value here. Passing an email creates an orphan wallet key that no other service reads.
user_id.title: User Id

params_schema.title: AdjustBalanceParams

return_schema.description:
get_user_balance / list_user_balances wallet entity (kind='userbalance').

``balance`` is a token count (int), NOT a currency Decimal, so the
``money.balance`` (Balanced) facet is deliberately NOT mixed in to avoid
int->Decimal type drift; kept as the existing plain int field.

list_user_balances wallet items use only ``{user_id, balance}``;
get_user_balance additionally returns ``available``, ``holds``,
``holds_total`` — all kept verbatim below.
return_schema.title: UserBalanceRecord

tools.name: billing_health
tools.description: Check billing system health: stream, consumers, pending lag.

params_schema.description:
Federal V17 placeholder for handlers that take no parameters.

`@chat.function` MUST declare a Pydantic params model — this is the
canonical empty model reused by read-only handlers (list_*, get_*,
*_health) that don't need any input.
params_schema.title: EmptyParams

return_schema.description:
billing_health return shape — a single billing-health entity
(kind='billinghealth').

Field names match the REAL runtime keys (verified vs
handlers_billing.fn_billing_health): {stream_length, first_entry_id,
last_entry_id, consumer_groups, pending_total, consumers, healthy}. The old
BillingHealthResponse keys (redis / lua_scripts / wallets / holds / issues)
were PHANTOM and are removed.
return_schema.title: BillingHealthRecord

tools.name: payment_config_get
tools.description: Get current payment provider configuration.

return_schema.description:
payment_config_get return shape — a single payment-config entity
(kind='paymentconfig').

Field names match the REAL runtime keys verbatim (verified vs
handlers_payment.fn_payment_config_get): {configured, enabled, mode,
has_publishable_key, has_webhook_secret}. id = title = 'payment_config'.
return_schema.title: PaymentConfigRecord

tools.name: payment_test_connection
tools.description: Test Stripe API connection with current keys.

return_schema.description:
payment_test_connection return shape — a single connection-test entity
(kind='paymenttest').

Field names match the REAL runtime keys verbatim (verified vs
handlers_payment.fn_payment_test_connection): {connected, currency, amount}.
id = title = 'payment_test'.
return_schema.title: PaymentTestResultRecord

tools.name: review_app
tools.description: Approve or reject a developer app submission

return_schema.description:
Receipt entity for review_app (kind='appreview').

Mirrors the ACTUAL handler return keys verbatim (verified vs
handlers_developer.review_app, which was made symmetric to these keys):
{app_id, action, status, reason, registered}. id = app_id; title = app_id.
return_schema.title: AppReviewReceipt

tools.name: review_payout
tools.description: Approve or reject a developer payout request

return_schema.description:
Receipt entity for review_payout (kind='payout').

Mirrors the ACTUAL handler return keys verbatim (verified vs
handlers_developer.review_payout, which was made symmetric to these keys):
{payout_id, action, note, status}. id = str(payout_id);
title = 'payout <id>'.
return_schema.title: PayoutReviewReceipt
